data.title: 10x Research: Bitcoin dollar-cost averaging strategies may have become ineffective; dynamic allocation based on market cycles performs better.
data.description: 10x Research published an article on its X platform stating that as Bitcoin has grown into a trillion-dollar asset, its return characteristics have shifted from early exponential growth to more pronounced cyclical fluctuations. The effectiveness of traditional dollar-cost averaging (DCA) strategies is diminishing. While Bitcoin has experienced significant volatility over the past five years, overall incremental returns have been limited. Passive holding strategies have faced multiple drawdowns and long-term returns that are mismatched. In contrast, dynamic allocation strategies based on market cycles perform better. This difference stems primarily from the fact that dynamic allocation can avoid significant drawdowns during structural bear markets and re-enter the market when conditions improve. In the current volatile market environment, risk management itself is a source of excess returns. A cycle-based allocation framework helps investors protect capital during bear markets and seize opportunities when the odds of success increase.

data.title: XRP Faces Resistance as It Trades at $1.32
data.description: XRP is currently trading at $1.32 as of April 3, navigating within a descending channel. According to NS3.AI, the first significant resistance level for a breakout is at $1.36. Data from Glassnode indicates resistance clusters between $1.34–$1.35 and $1.45–$1.47. Additionally, XRP's seven-day correlation with Bitcoin is reported to be 0.75.

data.title: Strategy's Bitcoin Acquisition Surpasses Newly Mined Volume
data.description: Strategy's perpetual preferred stock, STRC, is estimated to have acquired approximately $741 million worth of Bitcoin, equivalent to 4,535.58 BTC, over the past four trading days. According to NS3.AI, BitcoinTreasuries.net reported that this volume exceeds more than 2.5 times the amount of newly mined Bitcoin during the same period. The purchasing activity is believed to be driven by investor inflows seeking dividends ahead of the ex-dividend date, with precise figures anticipated in a regulatory filing on April 6.

data.title: Algorand Surges 13% Following Google Research Paper Highlighting Post-Quantum Protocols
data.description: Algorand experienced a significant increase of approximately 13% in the past 24 hours, reaching $0.12. According to NS3.AI, this surge follows a Google research paper that highlighted the network's implementation of post-quantum protocols in real-world scenarios. CoinGecko data indicates that Algorand has risen over 44% in the past week. Google's paper, dated March 31, identified Algorand as an example of post-quantum cryptography on a blockchain otherwise vulnerable to quantum threats. Analysts attribute this price movement to a renewed focus on the risks associated with quantum computing.

data.title: Security Incidents Surge in March 2026 with $39.8 Million Losses
data.description: In March 2026, a total of 46 security incidents were recorded, resulting in losses amounting to approximately $39.8 million, according to Foresight News. This marks the highest number of security incidents since November 2024, as monitored by CertiK. The firm noted that after reaching a low point in the third quarter of 2025, security incidents increased again in the fourth quarter of 2025 and the first quarter of 2026. Over the past two quarters, there has been a growing trend of code vulnerabilities being exploited.

data.description: 1. Turkey sells $20 billion worth of gold in three weeks to back its fiat currency, the lira; 2. STRC expects to raise enough funds this week to purchase 4,535 BTC; 3. Binance Wallet will launch the Sentio Pre-TGE event on April 7; 4. The Ethereum Foundation staked another $46.64 million in ETH, bringing the total staked amount to $96.59 million.

data.title: Cathie Wood Predicts Stability for Bitcoin Amid Institutional Growth
data.description: Cathie Wood, CEO of Ark Invest, expressed confidence that Bitcoin will not experience another decline of over 80%. According to NS3.AI, Wood attributes this stability to increased institutional participation, which has contributed to the maturation of the market. She emphasized that Bitcoin is now considered a stable store of value, reflecting its evolving role in the financial landscape.

data.title: RootData's Project Claim Feature Boosts Transparency and Engagement
data.description:
RootData has seen a significant increase in the number of projects joining its platform since the announcement of its project claim feature. According to ChainCatcher, over 20 well-known projects, including Bitway, Flock, Morph, and Solv, have joined this week alone. Recent data indicates that projects completing certification and updating their information have seen their transparency scores rise by more than 30%, leading to a 220% increase in their popularity within the RootData platform. This surge has greatly enhanced community trust and market attention.

Currently, RootData has certified over 120 projects. The project claim feature allows teams to manage and update project details directly on dedicated pages, including token economic models, investors, team members, and calendar events. This functionality enhances the accuracy and comprehensiveness of information, providing more precise and high-quality exposure on RootData and its network of over 220 data partners.

data.title: Bitget CandyBomb: Trade any cryptocurrency to unlock a 3 XAUT airdrop.
data.description: Bitget has launched a new round of CandyBomb, with a total prize pool of 3 XAUT. New users can earn up to 0.03 XAUT by completing tasks such as net deposit and their first contract trade. Detailed rules have been published on the official Bitget platform. Eligible users can click the "Join Now" button to register and participate in the event. The event ends on April 17th at 18:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: Gate Square's April Creator Incentive Program is now live! New users who post their first thread will receive a bonus.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Gate Square's April Creator Incentive Program is now live. During the event, posting and interacting on Gate Square will randomly trigger a red envelope rain reward. New users are guaranteed to receive a red envelope for their first post. The more posts you make, the higher the quality of your content, and the more active your interaction, the richer the rewards, with a single red envelope reaching up to 10 USDT. In addition, sharing the event on Gate Square or X will earn you a 200 USDT reward for being among the top 20 most viewed posts. Participating in the Creator Posting Leaderboard Challenge also gives you a chance to win a Gate 13th Anniversary Limited Edition Gift Box, a Gate × Redbull collaboration jacket, and token rewards, among other amazing prizes.

data.title: Solana Protocols Hit by $280 Million Exploit, Drift Initiates Contact with Attacker
data.description: Drift has reached out to wallets linked to a significant exploit, estimated by external firms to be between $280 million and $286 million. According to NS3.AI, Drift communicated from its Ethereum address to four wallets containing the stolen Ether, requesting the attacker to engage via Blockscan chat. SolanaFloor reported that the exploit impacted at least 20 Solana protocols, including Gauntlet, with losses estimated at $6.4 million. Cyvers noted that no funds had been recovered 48 hours post-attack, suggesting the incident might have been a staged operation.

data.title: Shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz have caused global shipping costs to skyrocket, with freight rates currently increasing by 11-12 times.
data.description: The Middle East conflict has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, causing a sharp rise in global shipping costs and a rapid increase in war risk insurance premiums for ships. Furthermore, shipping companies also need to consider freight rates, which industry insiders say have already increased 11 to 12 times. The cascading costs of insurance and detours are continuing to impact global trade and supply chain security. (CCTV Finance)

data.title: Middle East Conflict Disrupts Shipping in Hormuz Strait, Raising Global Costs
data.description: On April 3, shipping in the Hormuz Strait faced disruptions due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to BlockBeats, this situation has led to a sharp increase in global shipping costs, with war risk insurance premiums for vessels rising rapidly. Additionally, shipping companies are experiencing significant freight rate hikes, with industry experts noting that rates have surged by 11 to 12 times. The compounded costs of insurance and rerouting are continuously impacting global trade and supply chain security.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot CVD Analysis Highlights Trade Size Dynamics
data.description: According to NS3.AI, the recent analysis of the BTC/USDT spot CVD chart provides insights into the dynamics of buy and sell orders based on trade size. The CVD indicator, which is used to track these orders, offers a detailed view of market activity. The volume heatmap complements this by visually representing the intensity of trades, helping to identify patterns and trends in the cryptocurrency market. This analysis is crucial for understanding the behavior of traders and the potential impact on Bitcoin's price movements.

data.title: Bitget launches VIP Fast Track Program, breaking down the barriers to static holdings.
data.description: Bitget launched its VIP Fast Track Program today, breaking away from the traditional exchange upgrade rules that primarily rely on static holding thresholds. It adds three independent advancement paths: Contracts, Spot, and Holding, directly linking VIP progress to users' actual trading behavior. Each upgrade milestone is accompanied by a settlement-based reward mechanism, allowing users to instantly claim rewards upon reaching the corresponding milestones. These rewards offset trading costs incurred during the upgrade process, further reducing the barrier and friction between temporary trial access and the official VIP level. This event also marks the first phase of the UEX VIP season. Simultaneously, Bitget has also launched a VIP progress details page within its app, allowing users to view their upgrade progress in real time via mobile. This page dynamically calculates the eligibility criteria and displays the corresponding benefits for each level, including fee reductions and airdrop rewards, further enhancing the transparency of the VIP system.

data.title: UAE Denies Involvement in Alleged U.S. Military Strikes on Iran
data.description: On April 3, a spokesperson for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Ministry of Defense, Humaydi, stated in a media interview that UAE territory was not used for any attacks on Iran. According to BlockBeats, Humaydi firmly denied allegations that the U.S. military launched strikes on Iran from bases within the UAE. He emphasized that the UAE remains focused on defense while "reserving all military options." Humaydi also asserted that the UAE will not allow its sovereignty and security to be compromised and will take all necessary measures to counter threats.

data.title: Ireland Tests Digital Wallet with Age Verification to Protect Youth Online
data.description: Ireland has initiated the testing phase of its digital wallet, which includes an age verification feature aimed at enhancing the safety of teenagers in the online environment. According to Jin10, a statement from the Department of Public Expenditure and Reform indicates that this digital ID will support age verification, helping to shield children and young people from potential dangers on social media platforms. Ireland, home to the European headquarters of major global tech companies, is among several countries considering restrictions on social media use by teenagers. In January, Australia decided to ban users under 16 from opening accounts. Ireland views social media age verification as a step towards a broader ban, which could include platforms operated by companies like Meta. Earlier this year, Finance Minister Simon Harris described teenage social media use as a "significant public health issue," highlighting its impact on mental health.

data.title: Polymarket Secures Exclusive Prediction Market Rights with LaLiga in U.S. and Canada
data.description: Polymarket has entered into a multi-year agreement with Spain's LaLiga, granting exclusive prediction market rights in the United States and Canada. According to NS3.AI, the deal encompasses broadcast visibility during matches, digital programming, fan experiences, and the utilization of LaLiga's intellectual property for match-related markets. This partnership aims to enhance fan engagement and expand Polymarket's presence in the sports prediction market.

data.title: Iran launches 92nd wave of strikes
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced today (April 3) that in the 92nd wave of Operation True Commitment 4, it launched ballistic missiles and drones in multiple joint attacks against US and Israeli radar systems and maritime facilities in the region. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Stablecoin Transactions Surpass ACH Network Volume in February 2026
data.description:
Stablecoin transactions reached a significant milestone in February 2026, with monthly volumes hitting $7.2 trillion, surpassing the $6.8 trillion processed by the U.S. Automated Clearing House (ACH) network. According to ChainCatcher, Artemis data highlights the growing role of stablecoins as a global payment infrastructure, offering transactions without the need for banks, operating across weekends, and transcending borders.

In March, stablecoin transaction volumes continued to rise, reaching $7.5 trillion. During the first quarter of 2026, the total supply of stablecoins amounted to $315 billion, accounting for 75% of the total cryptocurrency transaction volume. Analysts from Standard Chartered Bank predict that by 2028, the total market capitalization of stablecoins will reach $2 trillion. The head of content at trading firm GSR warns that banks and fintech companies that overlook the explosive growth in this sector may face significant challenges.
